basil.label: Liturgy of St. Basil the Great
occasions: Sundays of Great Lent (except Palm Sunday)
Eves of Nativity and Theophany (or their days if on Sunday/Monday)

blocks.label: Opening Doxology
blocks.text_incipit: Blessed is the Kingdom of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit…

typical.text_incipit: Bless the Lord, O my soul… (Ps. 102)

feasts.note: Certain Great Feasts prescribe unique antiphons from the Menaion or Triodion

typical.text_incipit: Praise the Lord, O my soul… (Ps. 145)

feasts.note: Certain Great Feasts prescribe unique antiphons from the Menaion or Triodion

blocks.label: Only-Begotten Son
blocks.text_incipit: Only-begotten Son and immortal Word of God…

blocks.label: Third Antiphon
blocks.rubric: The Beatitudes (Matt 5:3-12) serve as the Third Antiphon on Sundays and most days when the Liturgy is served.

assembly.description: Beatitudes verses are interspersed with troparia from appointed sources. The calendar entry specifies sources and counts.

format.note: The fixed Beatitudes text (Matt 5:3-12) provides the verse framework
Calendar entry specifies: source (Octoechos, Triodion, Menaion, Canon ode), count, and tone

sunday.text_incipit: Come, let us worship and fall down before Christ… O Son of God, who art risen from the dead, save us who sing to Thee: Alleluia!

weekday.text_incipit: Come, let us worship and fall down before Christ… O Son of God, who art wondrous in Thy saints, save us who sing to Thee: Alleluia!

feasts.note: Great Feasts have unique entrance hymns

assembly.description: Ordered sequence of troparia as specified by the calendar entry. On Sundays, the Resurrectional Troparion of the tone is always first.
Ordered sequence of kontakia. The final kontakion uses 'Glory… now and ever…' as its prefatory connector.

typical.text_incipit: Holy God, Holy Mighty, Holy Immortal, have mercy on us.

substitutions.text: Before Thy Cross, we bow down in worship, O Master, and Thy holy Resurrection, we glorify.

substitutions.text_incipit: As many as have been baptized into Christ, have put on Christ. Alleluia.

blocks.label: Homily
blocks.rubric: The sermon is typically delivered after the Gospel reading.

blocks.label: Litany for the Departed
blocks.condition: Outside Sundays of Paschaltide; also omitted on certain feasts. On Sundays and weekday commemorations of the departed.

blocks.label: Cherubic Hymn
blocks.text_incipit: Let us who mystically represent the Cherubim…

great-thursday.label: Mystical Supper Hymn
great-thursday.text_incipit: Of Thy Mystical Supper, O Son of God…

great-saturday.label: Great Saturday Cherubic
great-saturday.text_incipit: Let all mortal flesh keep silence…

blocks.label: The Creed
blocks.text_incipit: I believe in one God, the Father Almighty…

basil.label: Anaphora of St. Basil the Great

sub_sections.label: Dialogue (Sursum Corda)
sub_sections.note: Priest–people exchange opening the Anaphora: 'The grace of our Lord…', 'Let us lift up our hearts…', 'Let us give thanks…'

sub_sections.label: Preface
sub_sections.note: Thanksgiving prayer; diverges between Chrysostom and Basil

sub_sections.label: Sanctus
sub_sections.note: People: 'Holy, holy, holy, Lord of Sabaoth…'

sub_sections.label: Words of Institution
sub_sections.note: Narrative of the Last Supper; people respond 'Amen' twice

sub_sections.label: Anamnesis & Oblation
sub_sections.note: 'Offering Thee Thine own, of Thine own…' / People: 'We praise Thee, we bless Thee…'

sub_sections.label: Epiklesis
sub_sections.note: Invocation of the Holy Spirit upon the Gifts; climax of the Anaphora. Diverges between Chrysostom and Basil.

sub_sections.label: Commemorations (Diptychs)
sub_sections.note: Theotokos, saints, bishop, living and departed

typical.text_incipit: It is truly meet to bless thee, O Theotokos…

substitutions.text_incipit: All of creation rejoices in thee, O Full of Grace…

substitutions.note: Each Great Feast has its own appointed irmos from the 9th ode of the canon

blocks.label: Elevation
blocks.rubric: The priest elevates the Holy Gifts.
blocks.text_incipit: Holy Things for the holy! / One is Holy, One is Lord, Jesus Christ, to the glory of God the Father. Amen.

sunday.text_incipit: Praise the Lord from the heavens, praise Him in the highest. Alleluia.

blocks.label: Pre-Communion Prayer
blocks.rubric: Said quietly by the faithful during the Communion Hymn, before approaching to receive.

blocks.label: Post-Communion Blessing
blocks.text_incipit: Save, O God, Thy people, and bless Thine inheritance.

blocks.label: We Have Seen the True Light

typical.text_incipit: We have seen the true Light. We have received the heavenly Spirit…

substitutions.text_incipit: Christ is risen from the dead, trampling down death by death…

blocks.label: Hymn of Thanksgiving
blocks.text_incipit: Let our mouths be filled with Thy praise, O Lord…

blocks.label: Blessed Be the Name of the Lord
blocks.text_incipit: Blessed be the name of the Lord, henceforth and forevermore. (3x)

blocks.label: Psalm 33
blocks.rubric: Read by the reader while the priest reads the Prayer of Thanksgiving behind the altar.
blocks.text_incipit: I will bless the Lord at all times; His praise shall continually be in my mouth…

blocks.label: Dismissal Troparia

chrysostom.text_incipit: Grace shining forth from thy lips like a beacon has enlightened the universe…

basil.text_incipit: Thy proclamation has gone out into all the earth…

blocks.note: Followed by Glory… now and ever… then Theotokion ('O protection of Christians…') or festal kontakion override from calendar.

assembly.description: The dismissal is built from multiple variable clauses.

parts.rule: Sunday: 'May He Who rose from the dead…'; weekday: 'May Christ our true God…'; feasts have unique openings
Names the Theotokos, Apostles, liturgy saint (Chrysostom or Basil), day-of-week patron, and saints from calendar
Always ends: '…have mercy on us and save us, forasmuch as He is good and loveth mankind.'
